meta.name: Fry Sight Words
meta.description: The 1000 most common words found in books and stories. Great for building reading fluency at every grade level.

k.definition: Points to one specific thing.
Shows what something belongs to.
Joins two things together.
A word we say before things.
Moving toward a place.
Inside something.
A linking word.
The person you are talking to.
Points to a specific thing.
A word used for a thing.
A word used for a boy.
A word about something before now.
Meant for someone.
Resting on top of something.
sentences: The cup is on the table.

k.definition: A word about more than one.
Used when comparing two things.
sentences: She runs as fast as me.

k.definition: Together with someone.
Belonging to a boy or man.
A word about more than one person.
A word for yourself.
Tells you where something is.
To exist or happen.
sentences: I want to be a reader.

k.definition: Points to something close to you.
To own or hold something.
Tells where something started.
sentences: I got a gift from him.

k.definition: Shows a choice between things.
The number 1.
Owned or held something before.
Next to something or who made it.
sentences: The dog sat by the door.

k.definition: A group of letters that means something.
Shows two different ideas.
The opposite word.
A question asking about a thing.
Every single one of them.
A word about a group in the past.
You and others together.
Asks about what time something happens.
sentences: Tell me when you are ready.
She smiled when she heard it.

k.definition: Something that belongs to you.
Able to do something.
Spoke words out loud before.
In a place away from you.
To do something with a tool.
sentences: She showed him how to use it.

k.definition: Used before a vowel sound.
Every one separately.
Asks you to choose between things.
sentences: She picked which book to read.
Which path goes to the park?

k.definition: A word used for a girl.
To perform an action.
Asks about the way something is done.
Belonging to a group of people.
Used when something might happen.
sentences: If it rains we stay in.
She asked if she could help.
Call me if you need anything.

k.definition: Something going to happen soon.
Moving to a higher place.
A different one from this one.
sentences: She sat on the other side.

k.definition: Having to do with a topic.
Moving away from inside.
A large number of things.
After that what happened next.
Refers to a group of people.
Points to more than one thing close to you.
Shows a very big amount.
sentences: I am so proud of you.

k.definition: A few but not all of them.
Belonging to a girl or woman.
Something that might possibly happen.
To build or create something new.
To feel happy about something.
Refers to a boy or man.
Moving to the inside of something.
When something happens measured in hours and minutes.
To own or hold something right now.
To use your eyes to see.
The number 2.
A greater amount than before.
To form letters and words on paper.
To move from one place to another.
To use your eyes to look.
sentences: She went to see the show.

k.definition: A word that shows how many.
The opposite of yes.
A path or how something is done.
Something that was able to happen.
More than one person together.
Belonging to you.
Used to compare two different things.
sentences: He is taller than his dad.

k.definition: Before anyone or anything else.
The clear liquid we drink and swim in.
sentences: She poured water in the cup.

k.definition: When something happened over a period of time.
To say a name or use the phone.
sentences: She heard him call her name.

k.definition: A question asking about a person.
A slippery liquid used for cooking.
sentences: The pan needs a little oil.

k.definition: Belonging to an animal or thing.
At this very moment.
To look for something and get it.
sentences: She tried to find her glove.
He helped me find the way.

k.definition: A large distance from end to end.
sentences: The giraffe has a long neck.

k.definition: Moving toward a lower place.
sentences: She looked down at her feet.

k.definition: The time when the sun is out.
Something that happened before now.
To go and bring something back.
sentences: She will get it for you.

k.definition: To move toward someone.
sentences: She told him to come in.

k.definition: Built or created something before.
Asking if you are allowed to do something.
sentences: She asked if she may help.

k.definition: One piece of a bigger whole.

1.definition: Above something or all finished.
Made or bought just recently.
sentences: He made a new friend today.

1.definition: Something you hear with your ears.
sentences: She heard a loud sound outside.
The sound of rain is calming.

1.definition: To pick something up and bring it.
sentences: She will take it to school.
He had to take a deep breath.

1.definition: Just one and nothing more.
sentences: She was the only one left.
He had only a minute to spare.

1.definition: Very small in size.
To do something that takes effort.
sentences: They work well as a team.

1.definition: To have something in your mind.
sentences: She did not know what to do.

1.definition: A spot or location.
sentences: She found a quiet place to read.
Put it back in its place.

1.definition: Units of time with twelve months each.
sentences: She has read for many years.
He has lived here for three years.

1.definition: To make your home in a place.
sentences: She wants to live near the park.
We live in a cozy house.

1.definition: Another word for yourself.
The opposite of front or returning.
sentences: She looked back at the house.

1.definition: To hand something to another person.
sentences: She wanted to give a gift.

1.definition: The greatest amount of something.
A much larger amount than usual.
Something that comes later in time.
An object or idea.
Belonging to us together.
Only or something that just happened.
The word used to call someone.
sentences: He called her name out loud.

1.definition: Something nice or done well.
sentences: She is a very good reader.

1.definition: A group of words that makes a complete thought.
sentences: Start each sentence with a capital letter.

1.definition: A grown up boy.
sentences: The man read to the class.

1.definition: To use your brain to figure things out.
To speak words out loud.
sentences: She wanted to say thank you.

1.definition: Very good or very large.
Asks about what place.
sentences: She asked where they were going.

1.definition: To do good for someone who needs it.
sentences: He offered to help right away.

1.definition: Going from one side to the other.
sentences: She read through the whole book.

1.definition: A very large amount of something.
Earlier in time happening first.
sentences: He had never tried that before.

1.definition: A row or a long thin mark.
sentences: He was first in line today.

1.definition: Correct or the opposite of left.
sentences: She knew the right thing to do.

1.definition: Also or more than enough.
What a word says or unkind.
sentences: She was never mean to anyone.
That was a mean thing to say.

1.definition: Something around for a long time.
sentences: That is a very old book.

1.definition: One or some no matter which.
sentences: She did not want any more.

1.definition: Exactly alike with no differences.
sentences: They both said the same thing.

1.definition: To share information with someone.
sentences: He forgot to tell his mom.

1.definition: A male child.
sentences: She helped the boy find his dog.

1.definition: To go behind or come after.
sentences: The dog loves to follow us.

1.definition: Moved toward a place before now.
sentences: They came all the way from home.

1.definition: To really wish you had something.
sentences: She did not want to stop reading.

1.definition: To let someone see or learn something.
sentences: She offered to show him how.
He was excited to show the class.

1.definition: In addition to something else.
sentences: He also finished his project early.

1.definition: In a circle or on every side.
sentences: We walked around the whole block.

1.definition: The shape or look of something.
sentences: She saw the form of a bird.
Clouds form high in the sky.

1.definition: The number 3.
Little in size not big at all.
sentences: Even small acts of kindness help.

1.definition: To put something in a place.
sentences: He set a new record today.

1.definition: To place something in a spot.
sentences: He put his name on the paper.

1.definition: The last part of something.
sentences: The end was the best part.
She read to the very end.

1.definition: When someone performs an action.
sentences: He does his homework right away.

1.definition: One more of the same thing.
Doing something the right way.
sentences: He did well on his test.

1.definition: Big in size.
sentences: He read a large stack of books.

1.definition: Something you really need to do.
Very large in size.
sentences: He took one big step forward.

1.definition: Flat and smooth or also true.
sentences: She was kind to even the new kids.
The road was flat and even.

1.definition: Of that kind or so great.
sentences: You are such a good reader.
She had such a fun day.
It was such a great book.

1.definition: Explains the reason something happened.
sentences: She wore boots because it rained.
He was happy because it was Friday.

1.definition: To move in a new direction or take a chance.
sentences: She took a turn on the swing.
Turn left at the stop sign.

1.definition: In this place right where you are.
Asks for the reason something happened.
sentences: She asked why the sky is blue.

1.definition: To say a question to someone.
sentences: She did not want to ask.

1.definition: Moved to a place before now.
sentences: He went to get his coat.

1.definition: More than one man.
To look at words and understand them.
sentences: She can read chapter books now.
He read the whole thing himself.

1.definition: Something important that you must have.
sentences: She did not need any help.
He knew he would need a plan.

1.definition: The ground or solid earth.
sentences: She planted seeds in the land.
The plane was ready to land.

1.definition: Not the same as something else.
sentences: She chose a different book this time.

1.definition: The place where you live.
sentences: He could not wait to get home.

1.definition: Refers to yourself and others together.
sentences: The teacher read to us today.

1.definition: To go from one place to another.
sentences: She had to move her books.
The clouds slowly move across the sky.

1.definition: To make an effort to do something.
sentences: He knew it was worth one more try.

1.definition: Gentle caring and nice to others.
sentences: He did a kind thing today.

1.definition: The part of your body at the end of your arm.
sentences: He waved his hand to say hello.

1.definition: A drawing or photo of something.
sentences: He took a picture of the sunset.

1.definition: One more time repeated.
sentences: She tried again and got it right.

1.definition: To become different or make different.
sentences: She decided to change her plan.
He wanted to change the ending of his story.

1.definition: Away from or no longer on something.
sentences: He wiped the mud off his boots.

1.definition: To have fun with games or activities.
sentences: She plays with her dog every day.

1.definition: To write or say the letters of a word.
sentences: She learned to spell her whole name.
He practiced how to spell each word.

1.definition: What we breathe all around us.
sentences: She took a deep breath of cool air.

1.definition: Going far from a place.
A living creature like a dog or bird.
sentences: My favorite animal is a dolphin.
He drew an animal for his project.

1.definition: A building where people live.
sentences: We live in a cozy house.
She painted a picture of a house.
The bird built a house in the tree.

1.definition: A sharp tip or a specific spot.
sentences: He drew a point at the top of the star.

1.definition: One side of a sheet of paper in a book.
sentences: He drew a picture on the last page.

1.definition: A symbol that makes up words or a written message.
sentences: She got a letter from her pen pal.
The letter A starts many words.

1.definition: A female parent.
sentences: She loves her mother very much.
His mother made his favorite dinner.

1.definition: A reply to a question.
sentences: She knew the answer right away.
Raise your hand if you know the answer.

1.definition: Looked for something and got it.
sentences: She finally found the right page.
He found a shortcut through the park.

1.definition: Not moving or continuing to happen.
sentences: She is still reading her book.
He still had more pages to go.

1.definition: To get new knowledge or a new skill.
sentences: She loves to learn new things.
He worked hard to learn that skill.

1.definition: Something that would be a good idea to do.
sentences: She thought she should ask first.
He knew he should say sorry.

1.definition: The country where many of us live.
sentences: She learned about the history of America.
People from all over come to America.

1.definition: The whole earth and everything on it.
sentences: Books take you around the world.
She wants to see the whole world.
He learned about animals from around the world.

1.definition: A thought or plan in your mind.
sentences: His idea helped the whole group.
Reading gave her a new idea every day.

2.definition: Far up from the ground.
sentences: She reached the high shelf herself.
He jumped as high as he could.

2.definition: Each single one without missing any.
Close to something not far away.
sentences: The library is near our house.

2.definition: To put things together to make more.
sentences: She decided to add more details.
He wanted to add a new chapter.

2.definition: What we eat to keep our bodies strong.
sentences: He cooked food for the whole family.

2.definition: In the middle of two things.
sentences: She could not choose between the two.
He found the book between the cushions.

2.definition: Something that belongs to just you.
sentences: He was proud of his own work.

2.definition: Under or lower than something else.
sentences: She wrote her name below the title.
The fish swam below the surface.

2.definition: A large area of land with its own people and rules.
sentences: He wants to visit every country someday.

2.definition: A living thing that grows in soil.
sentences: She watched the plant grow taller.
He planted a seed and saw a plant grow.

2.definition: Coming at the end or most recent.
sentences: She finished last but tried hard.
He read the last chapter tonight.
The last piece of cake was hers.

2.definition: A place where children go to learn.
sentences: She walked to school every day.
He made his best friends at school.

2.definition: A male parent.
sentences: She misses her father when he travels.
His father taught him to ride a bike.

2.definition: To hold onto something and not give it away.
sentences: She tried to keep her room clean.
He made a promise he worked hard to keep.

2.definition: A tall plant with a trunk and branches.
sentences: She climbed the old oak tree.
He read his book under the big tree.

2.definition: Not ever not even one time.
sentences: He had never tasted anything so good.

2.definition: To begin something for the first time.
sentences: She was nervous but decided to start.
He pressed start on the timer.

2.definition: A large town where many people live.
sentences: She visited a big city for the first time.
He loved the energy of the city.

2.definition: The planet we live on.
sentences: We must care for the earth.
She learned about the layers of the earth.
He drew a picture of the whole earth.

2.definition: The parts of your body you use to see.
sentences: She has bright and kind eyes.
He opened his eyes to the surprise.

2.definition: Brightness that helps you see.
sentences: She turned on a small light.
He saw a light in the distance.

2.definition: An idea that came into your mind.
sentences: I thought about it all day.
He shared his thought with the class.

2.definition: The top part of your body where your brain is.
sentences: Put your thinking cap on your head.

2.definition: Below or beneath something.
sentences: She found her book under the pillow.
He hid the surprise under the table.

2.definition: A tale about people or events.
sentences: He read the story three times.

2.definition: Used your eyes to look before now.
sentences: She saw the most amazing sunset.
He saw the answer right away.

2.definition: The opposite of right or what stayed behind.
sentences: She left her book at home.
There is only one cookie left.

2.definition: A short way to say do not.
sentences: Don't give up on your dream.

2.definition: A small number of things.
sentences: He needed just a few more minutes.

2.definition: During a period of time.
sentences: He drew pictures while she read.

2.definition: Moving forward with something or someone.
sentences: She sang along to the song.
He walked along the river path.

2.definition: Something that could possibly happen.
Near to something or to shut something.
sentences: He sat close to the fire.

2.definition: A thing that is not named.
sentences: He heard something outside the door.

2.definition: To appear to be a certain way.
sentences: She did not seem worried at all.
It seems like a good plan.

2.definition: Coming right after this one.
sentences: He will go next after her.

2.definition: Difficult or solid and firm.
sentences: The problem was hard at first.
He pressed hard with his pencil.

2.definition: Not closed so you can go in.
sentences: Open your book to page one.
The door is open for everyone.

2.definition: One thing that shows what others are like.
sentences: He wrote a clear example sentence.

2.definition: To start something for the first time.
sentences: She could not wait to begin.
He took a breath and began.

2.definition: The time you are alive.
sentences: He wanted to make a difference in life.

2.definition: Every single time without ever missing.
sentences: He always holds the door open.

2.definition: Points to more than one thing far from you.
sentences: She wanted to read those books.

2.definition: Two things together not just one.
Thin flat sheets you write or draw on.
sentences: She filled a whole paper with ideas.
He folded the paper into a boat.

2.definition: With each other at the same time.
sentences: She and her friend worked together.

2.definition: Received or obtained something before.
sentences: I got exactly what I hoped for.

2.definition: A number of people or things together.
sentences: She joined a new group today.
He led the whole group carefully.

2.definition: Happening many times not just once.
sentences: We often go to the library together.

2.definition: To move your legs very fast.
sentences: She can run a whole mile.
He decided to run to catch up.

2.definition: Having great meaning or value.
sentences: She learned something very important today.
He knew this was an important choice.

2.definition: Up to a certain time then stopping.
sentences: He did not stop until he finished.

2.definition: More than one young person.
sentences: She read to the children every day.

2.definition: The area to the left or right of something.
sentences: He wrote on every side of the paper.

2.definition: The parts of your body you walk on.
sentences: He stood on his feet all day.
My feet are tired from walking.

2.definition: A vehicle people ride in.
sentences: She read in the car on the trip.
He washed the car on Saturday.

2.definition: A unit used to measure distance.
sentences: The park is one mile away.
He walked a mile to school each day.

2.definition: The dark time after the sun goes down.
sentences: She looked at stars every night.
He dreamed about adventures all night long.

2.definition: To move your feet one step at a time.
sentences: We walk to school each day.
She loves to walk in the rain.
He decided to walk instead of run.

2.definition: The lightest color like snow.
A large body of salty water.
sentences: She saw dolphins in the sea.
He read a story set by the sea.

2.definition: Started something before now.
sentences: She began to read right away.
He began the story with a question.
It began to snow after lunch.

2.definition: To get bigger or taller over time.
sentences: He wanted to grow taller this year.

2.definition: Picked something up or brought it.
sentences: He took a long deep breath.
I took my time on the project.

2.definition: A long flowing body of fresh water.
sentences: The river flows by the town.
She saw fish in the river.
He skipped rocks across the river.

2.definition: The number 4.
To hold something and move with it.
sentences: Can you carry this for me?
She offered to carry his bag.
He had to carry everything up the hill.

2.definition: A region of a country or the condition of something.
sentences: She moved to a new state.
What state do you live in?
He described the state of the project.

2.definition: One time or long ago.
sentences: Once upon a time lived a dragon.
He visited once and never forgot it.

2.definition: Pages bound together with a story inside.
sentences: I love this book so much.
She read the whole book in one day.
He picked out a book at the library.

2.definition: To use your ears to pick up sounds.
sentences: She leaned in close to hear better.
He tried to hear every word.

2.definition: To not move or end something.
sentences: She did not want to stop reading.
The rain finally decided to stop.

2.definition: Not having something.
sentences: Do not go without your coat.

2.definition: Coming right after the first.
sentences: He read the second chapter quickly.
Give me just one second please.

2.definition: After the expected time.
sentences: She stayed up a little late reading.
He arrived late but caught up fast.

2.definition: To feel sad when someone is gone or to fail to get something.
sentences: She did not want to miss a single word.
He tried hard not to miss the catch.

2.definition: To chew and swallow food.
sentences: She likes to eat fruits and vegetables.
He read while waiting to eat.

2.definition: As much as you need no more.
sentences: She had enough time to finish.
He saved enough money to buy a book.
There was just enough light to read.

2.definition: Simple with nothing extra.
sentences: She wore a plain white shirt.
He wanted a plain piece of bread.
The plain answer was the best one.

2.definition: A female child.
sentences: She was the girl who loved books most.
A little girl found a magic door.

2.definition: Normal or what happens most of the time.
sentences: She did her usual morning reading.
He woke up earlier than usual today.
It was a usual day until something happened.

2.definition: Not old just starting out in life.
sentences: She is young but very wise.
Even young readers can tackle big books.
He learned to read when he was very young.

2.definition: Prepared and able to begin.
sentences: She was always ready before anyone else.
He got ready quickly and headed out.

2.definition: Higher than something else.
sentences: She hung her art above her desk.
The bird flew above the treetops.

2.definition: At any time at all.
sentences: Best book I have ever read.
Have you ever seen a shooting star?
She tried harder than she ever had before.

2.definition: The color of apples and fire trucks.
sentences: The red leaves were so beautiful.
He drew a bright red sun.

2.definition: A set of things written down in order.
sentences: She made a list of books to read.
He checked each item off the list.

2.definition: Even if or despite something.
sentences: She kept going though it was hard.
He smiled though he was tired.
Though it rained we had fun.

2.definition: To sense something with touch or emotion.
sentences: She could feel the excitement in the room.
He reached out to feel the soft fur.

2.definition: To speak words to someone.
sentences: Let us talk about the book.
She loves to talk about her stories.
He talked about his favorite character.

2.definition: An animal with wings feathers and a beak.
sentences: A bird sang outside my window.
She drew a colorful bird in her notebook.
He watched the bird build its nest.

2.definition: Something happening in a little while.
sentences: She will be here very soon.

2.definition: All the parts of a person or animal.
sentences: Keep your body strong and healthy.
She learned about the human body.
His whole body felt tired after the race.

2.definition: A common furry pet that barks.
sentences: My dog loves to read with me.
She trained her dog to sit and stay.
He drew a portrait of his dog.

2.definition: The people you are related to and love.
sentences: I love spending time with family.
She read to her whole family.
He made a scrapbook about his family.

2.definition: Straight or to be in charge of something.
sentences: She gave a direct and clear answer.
He helped direct the school play.

2.definition: A position or to ask a question.
sentences: She chose to pose a great question.
He held a heroic pose for the photo.

2.definition: Easy to do or understand.
sentences: She explained it in a simple way.
He solved it with a simple idea.

2.definition: Having a lot of power or energy.
sentences: He had a strong and steady voice.

2.definition: From one side to the other side.
sentences: He ran across the field to catch the ball.
Look both ways before walking across the street.

3.definition: Words and music you sing together.
sentences: He wrote a song about adventure.
The whole class sang the same song.

3.definition: To find the size or amount of something.
sentences: She used a ruler to measure the line.
He learned to measure ingredients for baking.

3.definition: An opening you go through to enter a place.
sentences: She knocked on the door three times.
He held the door open for everyone.

3.definition: Something that is made or created.
sentences: They sold their product at the fair.
She was proud of the product she made.
A great product takes time and effort.

3.definition: The darkest color like the night sky.
sentences: The black cat sat in the window.
He used black ink for his drawing.

3.definition: Not tall or not long.
sentences: He is short but very fast.
A short story can have a big message.

3.definition: A symbol that stands for a number.
sentences: She matched each numeral to its value.
He learned that each numeral has a name.

3.definition: A group of students who learn together.
sentences: She presented to the whole class.
He made a friend in his new class.

3.definition: Moving air that you can feel.
sentences: She felt the cool wind on her face.
He flew his kite in the strong wind.

3.definition: Something you ask when you want to know more.
sentences: She raised her hand with a great question.
He wrote a question at the end of his story.

3.definition: To take place or occur.
sentences: She wondered what would happen in the end.
He could not believe what happened.

3.definition: Finished with nothing missing.
sentences: He was thrilled to complete the challenge.
Make sure each sentence is complete.

3.definition: A large boat that travels on the sea.
sentences: She read a story about a ship.
He built a model ship for his project.

3.definition: A section or region of a place.
sentences: She explored the whole area of the park.
He cleaned his area of the classroom.

3.definition: One of two equal parts.
sentences: She ate half of her sandwich.
He finished half the book already.

3.definition: A hard piece of stone.
sentences: He sat on a large flat rock.
The rock by the river was smooth.

3.definition: The way things are arranged or a request.
sentences: She read them in the right order.
He placed an order for the new book.

3.definition: The bright hot flame from burning.
sentences: She watched the fire crackle and glow.
He made sure the fire was safe.

3.definition: One of the four directions opposite of north.
sentences: She traveled south on her trip.
He drew a compass with south at the bottom.

3.definition: Something that is hard and needs a solution.
sentences: He asked for help with the problem.
Every good story has a problem to solve.

3.definition: One part of something larger.
sentences: She gave him a piece of her apple.
He wrote one piece every day.
Take just one piece at a time.

3.definition: Shared information with someone before now.
sentences: He told his teacher right away.
I told you it would work out.

3.definition: Had information in your mind before now.
sentences: He knew the ending before it came.
I knew she would figure it out.

3.definition: To go by or hand something to someone.
sentences: He tried to pass the difficult test.

3.definition: From a certain time until now.
sentences: She has read a lot since last year.
He has improved so much since then.
I have loved books since I was little.

3.definition: The highest part of something.
sentences: She reached the top of the hill.
He wrote his name at the top.
The view from the top was amazing.

3.definition: All of something with nothing left out.
sentences: He ate the whole bowl of soup.

3.definition: A male ruler of a country in stories.
sentences: The kind king read to his people.
She dressed as a king for the play.
He read a story about a wise king.

3.definition: The area beyond our sky where stars are.
sentences: She dreams of traveling to space.
He read every book about space he could find.

3.definition: Used your ears to pick up a sound before now.
sentences: He heard the answer and smiled.

3.definition: Better than everything or everyone else.
sentences: She read the best book of the year.
He gave his best effort every time.

3.definition: A unit of time with sixty minutes.
sentences: She reads for one hour daily.
He finished in less than an hour.
They practiced for a whole hour together.

3.definition: More good than something you are comparing to.
sentences: He felt so much better after resting.
Practice makes you better every time.

3.definition: Real and not made up.
sentences: She told a true tale about bravery.
He made sure every fact was true.

3.definition: While something else is happening.
sentences: She smiled during the whole story.
He took careful notes during the lesson.

3.definition: The number 100.
sentences: He counted to a hundred without stopping.
There were a hundred reasons to keep reading.

3.definition: The number 5.
sentences: She has read five chapters today.
He scored five goals at practice.
Give me five after you finish.

3.definition: To bring something back into your memory.
sentences: She tried to remember every word.
He could remember the story perfectly.

3.definition: One movement of your foot or a part of a plan.
sentences: Take one step at a time.
He explained every step to the class.

3.definition: Before the usual or expected time.
sentences: She woke up early to read.
He arrived early to get a good seat.
Starting early always helps you succeed.

3.definition: To keep something in your hands or arms.
sentences: Hold the book with both hands.
She offered to hold his spot in line.
He tried to hold onto every detail.

3.definition: One of the four directions where the sun sets.
sentences: The sun sets in the west.
She traveled west across the country.
He pointed west toward the mountains.

3.definition: The surface of the earth we walk on.
sentences: She sat on the ground and read.
He found a coin on the ground.

3.definition: A feeling of wanting to learn more about something.
sentences: She has a deep interest in science.
He showed great interest in every book.
Reading grows your interest in the world.

3.definition: To stretch out to get something.
sentences: She tried to reach the top shelf.
He worked hard to reach his reading goal.
Keep going until you reach the end.

3.definition: Moving at a high speed.
sentences: He ran fast to catch up with friends.
Time goes fast when you love what you do.

3.definition: An action word in a sentence.
sentences: She found the verb in every sentence.
He learned that every sentence needs a verb.

3.definition: To make music with your voice.
sentences: She loves to sing every morning.
He started to sing on the way home.
They all began to sing the same song.

3.definition: To pay careful attention to sounds.
sentences: She taught herself to listen closely.
He would listen to audiobooks every night.

3.definition: The number 8.
sentences: She has read eight books this month.
He practiced for eight whole days.

3.definition: The words people use to speak and write.
sentences: She loved learning a new language.
He used beautiful language in his writing.

3.definition: In the middle of a group.
sentences: She was among the top readers.
He found his favorite book among hundreds.
Among all the choices she picked the best one.

3.definition: The ability or strength to do something.
sentences: She had the power to change things.
He used his power to help others.

3.definition: A place smaller than a city where people live.
sentences: She grew up in a small town.
He knew every person in town.
The whole town came to the reading fair.

3.definition: Very good or perfectly okay.
sentences: He wrote in a fine clear hand.

3.definition: To operate a vehicle or strong determination.
sentences: She has a drive to succeed.
He watched his dad drive carefully.
Her drive to read helped her improve fast.

3.definition: A comfortable temperature not too hot or cold.
sentences: He loved warm cocoa on cold nights.
She curled up in her warm reading chair.

3.definition: Something that happens or is seen very often.
sentences: She used common sense to solve it.
He noticed a common pattern in the story.

3.definition: To carry something with you to a place.
sentences: She always remembered to bring her journal.
He asked if he could bring a friend along.

3.definition: To make something clear so others understand.
sentences: She was great at explain hard ideas simply.
He took his time to explain every step.

3.definition: Without water or moisture.
sentences: She found a dry spot to sit and read.
He dried his hands before picking up the book.

3.definition: An oval shell that holds a baby animal.
sentences: She found a bird egg in the grass.
He read a story where a dragon hatched from an egg.
An egg can hold so much life inside.

3.definition: To sit on something that carries you.
sentences: She loves to ride her bicycle.
He took a long ride through the park.
They went for a ride to the library.

3.definition: The smallest unit of life in a body.
sentences: Every living thing is made of cells.
She learned about the cell in science.
He drew a diagram of a plant cell.

3.definition: To feel sure that something is true.
sentences: She did not believe the story at first.
He worked hard to believe in himself.

3.definition: A part of a whole thing.
sentences: He solved the fraction problem quickly.
A fraction shows part of a whole.

3.definition: A large area covered with many trees.
sentences: She read a story set deep in the forest.
He hiked through the forest on his trip.

3.definition: Maybe or possibly.
sentences: She thought perhaps there was a better way.
Perhaps the best adventure is in a book.

3.definition: A long vehicle on tracks or to practice a skill.
sentences: She rode the train to the city.
He would train every day to improve.
The train of thought led to a great idea.

3.definition: Right with no mistakes.
sentences: She checked her work to make it correct.
He always tried to give the correct response.

3.definition: Having the power or skill to do something.
sentences: She was able to read the whole book.
He was finally able to reach his goal.
Practice makes you able to do amazing things.

3.definition: How old someone or something is.
sentences: Reading is great at any age.
She started reading at a very young age.
He was proud of what he learned at his age.

3.definition: The letters A, E, I, O, and U.
sentences: She circled every vowel she could find.
He learned that vowels make words flow.

3.definition: Moving in the direction of something.
sentences: He ran toward the finish line.
They moved toward a better answer.

3.definition: The bright star that gives us light and warmth.
sentences: The sun rose over the mountains.
She read outside in the warm sun.
He watched the sun slowly set.

3.definition: A drawing that shows where places are.
sentences: She drew a map of the school.
He studied the map before the trip.
A good map helps you find your way.

3.definition: To add the same number many times.
sentences: He practiced multiply facts every day.
To multiply is to make a number grow.

3.definition: Easy to see or understand.
sentences: He spoke in a clear and confident voice.
On a clear day you can see so far.

3.definition: The part of your face you breathe and smell with.
sentences: She wrinkled her nose at the smell.
He pointed to his nose and smiled.
The nose helps you enjoy the smell of fresh books.

3.definition: Before now in the past.
sentences: She read that book long ago.
He learned this word a week ago.
Not too long ago she could not read this well.

3.definition: Was standing in a place before now.
sentences: She stood at the front of the class.
He stood up to share his story.

3.definition: An aircraft that flies through the air.
sentences: She watched the plane fly overhead.
He read about a pilot and his plane.
The plane carried her to a new adventure.

3.definition: A set of parts that work together.
sentences: She created a system for tracking her reading.
He learned how the system of sounds works.

3.definition: At the back of something.
sentences: She hid behind the big chair.
He left his book behind by mistake.
Look behind you for a surprise.

3.definition: Moved very fast before now.
sentences: She ran all the way home.
He ran faster than ever before.
The puppy ran circles around us.

3.definition: Shaped like a circle or ball.
sentences: She drew a round sun in her picture.
He rolled the round stone down the hill.

3.definition: A small vessel that floats on water.
sentences: She read a story about a little boat.
He made a paper boat and floated it.
The boat rocked gently on the water.

3.definition: An activity played for fun with rules.
sentences: Reading can be a fun game.
She invented her own word game.
He played the spelling game with his class.

3.definition: A strong push or pull on something.
sentences: She used all her force to open the jar.
He learned about the force of gravity.
A story can have the force to change you.

3.definition: Carried something with you before now.
sentences: He brought an extra pencil just in case.
They brought snacks to share at reading time.

3.definition: To know the meaning of something clearly.
sentences: She worked hard to understand every word.
He asked questions to understand better.
Good readers try to understand as they go.

3.definition: Grew or became more advanced over time.
sentences: She developed into a strong reader.
He developed a love for science stories.
Great readers are developed through practice.

3.definition: A very large hill that rises high up.
sentences: She climbed the mountain in her story.
He read about a boy who lived near a mountain.
From the top of the mountain you can see everything.

3.definition: To use something sharp to divide an object.
sentences: He helped cut the ribbon at the opening.
Cut the story into sections to study it.

3.definition: The number 7.
sentences: She read seven books this month.
He practiced for seven days in a row.
There are seven days in every week.

3.definition: One of the four directions opposite of south.
sentences: Birds fly north in the spring.
She pointed north toward the mountains.
He drew north at the top of his map.

3.definition: One of the four directions where the sun rises.
sentences: The sun rises in the east.
She traveled east to visit family.
He pointed east toward the ocean.

3.definition: Something that repeats in a regular way.
sentences: She noticed a pattern in the words.
He drew a colorful repeating pattern.
Finding a pattern helps you solve problems faster.

3.definition: The outside or top layer of something.
sentences: The surface of the water was calm.
She wrote carefully on the smooth surface.
He studied the surface of the moon.

3.definition: To make or create something.
sentences: She worked hard to produce a great story.
He helped produce the school play.
Plants produce oxygen that we breathe.
